tier1.name: a2go-vllm-qwen3
tier1.description: Tier 1: Qwen3-30B-A3B on 1x H100 (~$2/hr) - Best for validation

tier2.name: a2go-vllm-mimo
tier2.description: Tier 2: MiMo-V2-Flash on 2x H100 (~$4/hr) - Fastest inference

tier3.name: a2go-vllm-glm47
tier3.description: Tier 3: GLM-4.7-FP8 on 4x H100 or 2x H200 (~$7-8/hr) - SOTA tool calling
dockerStartCmd: pip install -U vllm --pre && vllm serve ${MODEL_NAME} --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tensor-parallel-size ${TENSOR_PARALLEL_SIZE} --max-model-len ${MAX_MODEL_LEN} --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --speculative-config.method mtp --speculative-config.num_speculative_tokens 1 --tool-call-parser ${TOOL_CALL_PARSER} --reasoning-parser ${REASONING_PARSER} --enable-auto-tool-choice --api-key ${VLLM_API_KEY} --served-model-name ${SERVED_MODEL_NAME}

tier3_h200.name: a2go-vllm-glm47-h200
tier3_h200.description: Tier 3 Alt: GLM-4.7-FP8 on 2x H200 (~$7/hr) - Best value for SOTA
dockerStartCmd: pip install -U vllm --pre && vllm serve ${MODEL_NAME} --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tensor-parallel-size ${TENSOR_PARALLEL_SIZE} --max-model-len ${MAX_MODEL_LEN} --gpu-memory-utilization 0.90 --speculative-config.method mtp --speculative-config.num_speculative_tokens 1 --tool-call-parser ${TOOL_CALL_PARSER} --reasoning-parser ${REASONING_PARSER} --enable-auto-tool-choice --api-key ${VLLM_API_KEY} --served-model-name ${SERVED_MODEL_NAME}

notes.tier1: Start here to validate the setup. Qwen3-30B-A3B has 69.6% SWE-bench with only 3B active params.
notes.tier2: MiMo-V2-Flash offers 150 tok/s inference speed with 73.4% SWE-bench. MIT licensed.
notes.tier3: GLM-4.7 has SOTA tool calling (84.7% τ²-Bench) and 73.8% SWE-bench. Requires vLLM nightly.
notes.tier3_h200: Same as tier3 but uses 2x H200 instead of 4x H100. Slightly cheaper at $7.18/hr vs $7.96/hr.
